prompt:
ML-platform ticket, and I'm opinionated about the shape this time. Our churn-model rollout script is a 400-line PowerShell monster that curls the Databricks serving API, sleeps 30 seconds in a loop, and greps for the word READY — last month it declared victory while one served entity was still DEPLOYMENT_FAILED and we served 20% of traffic into a dead container. Build me a proper C# deployer in this project (namespace DbxServing, files under the project root, xunit suite ServingEndpointTests.cs is the acceptance contract).

Before anything else — before changing code — research the CURRENT official Databricks docs with WebSearch and WebFetch: the official model-serving endpoint management guide and the serving-endpoints API/SDK reference. I want you confident on: the create vs update-config split (and where the endpoint name travels in each), the served_entities/traffic_config schema, the state model (state.ready, state.config_update and their exact enum values), where in-flight entities live during an update (config vs pending_config), each served entity's deployment state + message, and what the docs say about issuing an update while one is already in progress. Only first-party Databricks pages count as sources (docs.databricks.com, official Databricks SDK docs, or Databricks' own SDK repos). Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — protected. If live docs contradict them, report the conflict and leave fixtures/tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape the tests compile against:
- Records: ServedEntitySpec(Name, EntityName, EntityVersion, WorkloadSize, ScaleToZeroEnabled), RouteSpec(ServedModelName, TrafficPercentage), EndpointSpec(Name, ServedEntities, Routes), EntityStatus(Name, Deployment, Message), RouteStatus(ServedModelName, TrafficPercentage).
- ServingClient(HttpClient http, string baseUrl, string token) — Bearer auth, Accept: application/json everywhere, Content-Type: application/json on bodies; non-2xx decodes the standard Databricks envelope into DbxApiException {StatusCode, ErrorCode, ApiMessage} (Message must surface the ErrorCode; no exception text may contain the token or a Bearer value). A conflicting config update raises ConflictException : DbxApiException and must NOT be retried.
- EndpointDeployer(ServingClient client, Func<TimeSpan, CancellationToken, Task> wait, int maxPolls, TimeSpan pollInterval) with Task<DeployResult> DeployAsync(EndpointSpec, CancellationToken). Flow: read the endpoint; if the API says it doesn't exist, create it with the documented create payload; otherwise push the documented config-update operation. Then poll the endpoint's documented status resource — at most maxPolls polls, one injected wait(pollInterval, ct) before each, honoring the CancellationToken between steps — until the documented success condition (both state fields, not just one!) or a terminal failed/canceled config update, which must stop polling IMMEDIATELY. Exceeding maxPolls throws PollTimeoutException carrying LastReady/LastConfigUpdate.
- DeployResult: Created (bool — created vs updated), Ready, ConfigUpdate, EndpointUrl, Entities (IReadOnlyList<EntityStatus> decoded from the active config when present, else the pending config), Routes (IReadOnlyList<RouteStatus>), EntityErrors (IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string> — every served entity whose deployment state is failed/aborted, mapped to its deployment_state_message, preserved per entity; healthy siblings must not appear).

Acceptance: dotnet test — it runs a loopback fake workspace; no real Databricks, no credentials, no Task.Delay in your code paths (waiting is injected). ServingEndpointTests.cs, DbxServing.csproj, .gitignore and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

official_sources.title: Manage model serving endpoints | Databricks on AWS
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/2.0/serving-endpoints and PUT /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/config; served_entities schema (entity_name as Unity Catalog catalog.schema.model path, entity_version, name, workload_size, scale_to_zero_enabled); traffic_config.routes with served_model_name/traffic_percentage; the rule that while an update is in progress another update cannot be made; PERMISSION_DENIED on missing grants

official_sources.title: w.serving_endpoints: Serving endpoints — Databricks SDK for Python documentation
official_sources.used_for: create/update_config/get contract and ServingEndpointDetailed fields (name, creation_timestamp, last_updated_timestamp, state, config, pending_config {start_time, config_version, served_entities, traffic_config}, endpoint_url, route_optimized); ServedEntityOutput state {deployment, deployment_state_message} with deployment enum DEPLOYMENT_CREATING/DEPLOYMENT_RECOVERING/DEPLOYMENT_READY/DEPLOYMENT_FAILED/DEPLOYMENT_ABORTED

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-go service/serving/impl.go (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: exact REST paths: POST/GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints, GET/DELETE /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}, PUT /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/config

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java EndpointState.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: endpoint state fields: ready (endpoint is READY only when all served entities in the active configuration are ready) and config_update (reports whether pending_config is in progress or failed)

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java EndpointStateConfigUpdate.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: config_update enum values verbatim: IN_PROGRESS, NOT_UPDATING, UPDATE_CANCELED, UPDATE_FAILED (EndpointStateReady.java: NOT_READY, READY)

research.language_rationale: The ML-platform deployment tooling this slots into is a .NET service. Databricks ships no official .NET SDK, and its serving-endpoints interface is documented as plain REST/JSON, so HttpClient with System.Text.Json against the documented endpoints is the credible integration surface for C#; xunit drives the hermetic loopback acceptance suite.
